summaryText: Guest Pastor Naeem Fazal shared his powerful testimony of growing up Muslim in Kuwait and encountering Jesus in a dramatic, supernatural way, leading to his conversion and call to ministry. He used his story to illustrate a central message from Luke 8:42-48: that followers of Christ are not meant to remain in the role of the woman with the issue, always defined by their problems, but are called to step into the identity of Jesus in the world—people from whom God's power flows. Pastor Naeem challenged the congregation to 'break up' with the person they used to be—the old identities, fears, and lies spoken over them—and to embrace who God created them to be. He explained that we often live in a story we didn't write, but through Christ, we are empowered to live from a place of divine purpose and authority. The sermon emphasized guarding the heart, as what is inside us flows out to affect others, and encouraged listeners to stop being led by fear and insecurity, which only hold us back. Instead, we are to put a 'leash' on our fears and be led by hope into the future God has prepared.
mainPoints: Believers are called to shift from being the person with the issues (like the woman in Luke 8) to being the person from whom God's power flows.
To step into God's purpose, we must 'break up' with our old identities, fears, and the lies we've believed about ourselves.
We must guard our hearts because what is inside—whether love or bitterness—flows out and impacts the world around us.

versesMentioned.text: As Jesus was on his way, the crowds almost crushed him. And a woman was there who had been subject to bleeding for twelve years, but no one could heal her. She came up behind him and touched the edge of his cloak, and immediately her bleeding stopped. 'Who touched me?' Jesus asked. When they all denied it, Peter said, 'Master, the people are crowding and pressing against you.' But Jesus said, 'Someone touched me; I know that power has gone out from me.' Then the woman, seeing that she could not go unnoticed, came trembling and fell at his feet. In the presence of all the people, she told why she had touched him and how she had been instantly healed. Then he said to her, 'Daughter, your faith has healed you. Go in peace.'
Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it.

Monday.scripture: Proverbs 4:23 (NIV) - Above all else, guard your heart, for everything you do flows from it.
Monday.action: Spend 5 minutes in quiet reflection. Identify one negative thought or fear you've allowed to reside in your heart today.
Monday.prayer: Lord, help me to be aware of what I'm allowing into my heart. Give me discernment to guard it for You.

Tuesday.scripture: Luke 8:46 (NIV) - But Jesus said, 'Someone touched me; I know that power has gone out from me.'
Tuesday.action: Intentionally do one small act of kindness today, believing God's love and power can flow through you to bless someone else.
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, remind me that Your power lives in me. Let my simple actions today be conduits of Your grace.

Wednesday.scripture: 2 Timothy 1:7 (NIV) - For the Spirit God gave us does not make us timid, but gives us power, love and self-discipline.
Wednesday.action: Identify one fear that consistently holds you back. Write it down on a piece of paper as a symbolic act of naming it.
Wednesday.prayer: God, Your Spirit is power, love, and self-discipline, not fear. I choose to trust You over my anxiety today.

Thursday.scripture: Ephesians 2:10 (NIV) - For we are God’s handiwork, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do.
Thursday.action: Reflect on one unique strength or passion God has given you. How can it be used to serve others?
Thursday.prayer: Father, thank you that I am Your handiwork. Show me the good works You have prepared for me to walk in.

Friday.scripture: Philippians 3:13-14 (NIV) - Brothers and sisters, I do not consider myself yet to have taken hold of it. But one thing I do: Forgetting what is behind and straining toward what is ahead...
Friday.action: Take a literal stick or piece of paper. Write an old identity or lie you're releasing (e.g., 'failure,' 'not enough'). Break or tear it as a prayerful act of surrender.
Friday.prayer: Jesus, I break my agreement with [name the lie]. I choose to forget what is behind and strain toward what You have ahead for me.

Saturday.scripture: Matthew 5:16 (NIV) - In the same way, let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.
Saturday.action: Look for one opportunity to be an encouragement or offer hope to someone, not as someone with issues, but as a representative of Christ's love.
Saturday.prayer: Holy Spirit, lead me to someone who needs encouragement today. Let my life point them to the Father's glory.
